title: Normalized Document
description: The unified output contract for ONE extracted document, regardless of whether the source was Word, PDF, or PowerPoint. 06_normalize.py MUST produce objects that validate against this schema. Downstream consumers rely on these keys and never need to know the original format. NOTE: keys are intentionally explicit; do not rename them.

doc_id.description: SHA-1 of the source file bytes. Used as the unique, collision-safe output filename so two sources named the same don't overwrite each other.

source_filename.description: Original filename as found in data/raw_docs/ (basename, with extension).

source_format.description: The MODERN format that was actually parsed.

was_converted_from.description: If the source was a legacy binary converted via LibreOffice, the original format; else null.

was_ocr.description: True if any text in this document came from OCR (scanned/image PDF). OCR text is lower confidence; see extraction.warnings.

metadata.description: Document-level metadata. Missing values are null, never omitted.

blocks.description: All content in READING ORDER. This ordering is the core quality guarantee — emit blocks in document/position order, never grouped by type. Tables also appear here (type='table') so their position is preserved.

tables.description: Convenience index of every table, duplicated from the blocks array, for consumers that only want tables.

full_text.description: All block text concatenated in reading order (join with double newline). The cheap field most consumers use. May be empty ONLY if the document is a known failure logged in failures.csv.

extractor.description: Which engine produced this. 'ocr' means scanned-PDF path.

warnings.description: Non-fatal issues, e.g. 'ocr_low_confidence', 'stripped 3 running headers', 'encrypted but opened with empty password'.

block_id.description: Sequential index within this document, in reading order.

type.description: Semantic role. 'notes' = PPTX speaker notes. 'table' blocks carry the table in the 'table' field and an empty 'text'.

level.description: Heading depth (1=H1) or list/bullet nesting depth; null when not applicable.

page.description: 1-based page for PDF/Word; null for pptx.

text.description: The block's text. Empty string for type='table' (data lives in 'table').

table.description: Populated only when type='table'; otherwise null.

rows.description: Row-major cells as strings. First row is often the header but this is NOT assumed — do not drop it.

examples.full_text:
Q3 Review

Revenue up 12%

Mention the EMEA outperformance.
